edges.description: Developmental transcriptional dysregulation contributes to impaired linear growth.
Developmental dysregulation contributes to delayed bone age.
Developmental dysregulation contributes to a recognizable craniofacial phenotype.
Developmental dysregulation contributes to characteristic skeletal anomalies.
Disrupted chromatin-associated developmental regulation contributes to neurocognitive impairment.
Neurodevelopmental dysregulation contributes to severe speech and language impairment.
Developmental dysregulation contributes to the characteristic hypernasal voice quality.
Developmental dysregulation contributes to the characteristic high-pitched voice quality.

nodes.description: Typical craniofacial features are a defining part of the Floating-Harbor syndrome phenotype.

evidence.snippet: SRCAP-related Floating-Harbor syndrome (SRCAP-FHS) is characterized by typical craniofacial features; low birth weight, normal head circumference, and short stature;

nodes.description: Brachydactyly is a representative skeletal anomaly in Floating-Harbor syndrome.

evidence.snippet: SRCAP-related Floating-Harbor syndrome (SRCAP-FHS) is characterized by typical craniofacial features; low birth weight, normal head circumference, and short stature; bone age delay that normalizes between ages six and 12 years; skeletal anomalies (brachydactyly, broad fingertips, clinodactyly, short thumbs, prominent joints, and clavicular abnormalities); severe receptive and expressive language impairment;

nodes.description: Delayed bone age is a classic diagnostic feature of Floating-Harbor syndrome.

evidence.snippet: SRCAP-related Floating-Harbor syndrome (SRCAP-FHS) is characterized by typical craniofacial features; low birth weight, normal head circumference, and short stature; bone age delay that normalizes between ages six and 12 years; skeletal anomalies (brachydactyly, broad fingertips, clinodactyly, short thumbs, prominent joints, and clavicular abnormalities); severe receptive and expressive language impairment;

nodes.description: Severe language impairment is one of the major developmental manifestations of Floating-Harbor syndrome.

evidence.snippet: SRCAP-related Floating-Harbor syndrome (SRCAP-FHS) is characterized by typical craniofacial features; low birth weight, normal head circumference, and short stature; bone age delay that normalizes between ages six and 12 years; skeletal anomalies (brachydactyly, broad fingertips, clinodactyly, short thumbs, prominent joints, and clavicular abnormalities); severe receptive and expressive language impairment;

nodes.description: Early intervention, special education, and related supportive services are recommended for developmental disability management.

evidence.snippet: Early intervention programs, special education, and vocational training to address developmental disabilities; communication rehabilitation with sign language or alternative means of communication; behavior management by a behavioral specialist / psychologist with consideration of medication as needed.

nodes.description: Recombinant human growth hormone has been used for short stature, but published evidence suggests only limited benefit in Floating-Harbor syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular Genetics and Pathogenesis of the Floating Harbor Syndrome: Case Report of Long-Term Growth Hormone Treatment and a Literature Review
evidence.snippet: We report the history of treatment of the proband with GH, which resulted in modest improvement in growth prior to puberty.

nodes.description: A high-pitched voice is a characteristic voice phenotype in Floating-Harbor syndrome.

evidence.snippet: SRCAP-related Floating-Harbor syndrome (SRCAP-FHS) is characterized by typical craniofacial features; low birth weight, normal head circumference, and short stature; bone age delay that normalizes between ages six and 12 years; skeletal anomalies (brachydactyly, broad fingertips, clinodactyly, short thumbs, prominent joints, and clavicular abnormalities); severe receptive and expressive language impairment; hypernasality and high-pitched voice; and intellectual disability that is typically mild to moderate.

nodes.description: Hypernasality is a characteristic voice and speech feature in Floating-Harbor syndrome.

evidence.snippet: SRCAP-related Floating-Harbor syndrome (SRCAP-FHS) is characterized by typical craniofacial features; low birth weight, normal head circumference, and short stature; bone age delay that normalizes between ages six and 12 years; skeletal anomalies (brachydactyly, broad fingertips, clinodactyly, short thumbs, prominent joints, and clavicular abnormalities); severe receptive and expressive language impairment; hypernasality and high-pitched voice; and intellectual disability that is typically mild to moderate.

nodes.description: Intellectual disability is usually mild to moderate in affected individuals.

evidence.snippet: SRCAP-related Floating-Harbor syndrome (SRCAP-FHS) is characterized by typical craniofacial features; low birth weight, normal head circumference, and short stature; bone age delay that normalizes between ages six and 12 years; skeletal anomalies (brachydactyly, broad fingertips, clinodactyly, short thumbs, prominent joints, and clavicular abnormalities); severe receptive and expressive language impairment; hypernasality and high-pitched voice; and intellectual disability that is typically mild to moderate.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular Genetics and Pathogenesis of the Floating Harbor Syndrome: Case Report of Long-Term Growth Hormone Treatment and a Literature Review
evidence.snippet: FHS is caused by heterozygous mutations in the SRCAP gene; however, little is known about the pathogenesis of FHS or the effectiveness of its treatment.
SRCAP | HGNC:16974 | Floating-Harbor syndrome | MONDO:0007621 | AD | Definitive

nodes.description: Floating-Harbor syndrome is caused by heterozygous truncating SRCAP variants. Available review evidence supports a dominant-negative model with disrupted chromatin-associated transcriptional control.

biological_processes: regulation of transcription by RNA polymerase II

evidence.reference_title: Molecular Genetics and Pathogenesis of the Floating Harbor Syndrome: Case Report of Long-Term Growth Hormone Treatment and a Literature Review
evidence.snippet: Finally, we suggested a new model of FHS pathogenesis which provides possible basis for the dominant negative nature of FHS-causing mutations and explains limited effects of GH treatment in FHS.

nodes.description: Postnatal short stature is a defining clinical feature of Floating-Harbor syndrome.

evidence.snippet: SRCAP-related Floating-Harbor syndrome (SRCAP-FHS) is characterized by typical craniofacial features; low birth weight, normal head circumference, and short stature;

nodes.description: Communication rehabilitation is recommended for the severe expressive and receptive language phenotype.

evidence.snippet: Early intervention programs, special education, and vocational training to address developmental disabilities; communication rehabilitation with sign language or alternative means of communication; behavior management by a behavioral specialist / psychologist with consideration of medication as needed.
